purpose: Recover the residual-blind schema of the 15-thalion ring and compare untwisted versus twisted G900 candidates.
boundary: Residual labels are not used in this pass.
This is schema recovery, not Gap A closure.
The 15-thalion ring is still a leading candidate, not a proved source map.
Later residual evaluation must use only features derived here or in a uniform lift.

schema_map.vertex_id_pattern: Tss_Sll where ss is thalion slot 0..14 and ll is local G60 state 0..59

interpretation.baseline_contrast: not the untwisted Cartesian product baseline

interpretation.main_result: The 15-thalion ring schema is recoverable without using Gap A residual labels.
interpretation.vertex_result: The source uses the product register Tss_Sll, giving 15 thalion slots and 60 local states per slot.
interpretation.edge_result: The candidate ring sources expose 3600 edges and an edge-kind/law basis suitable for internal-versus-carrier testing.
interpretation.twist_result: Untwisted and twisted candidates can be compared at the directed edge-set and edge-law level.
interpretation.next_question: Can all 18 WXYZTI role rows be lifted uniformly into this recovered ring schema?
